description: A polished White House-branded propaganda graphic in portrait format with a dark, dramatic palette. The upper half is a moody, slightly desaturated photo of President Trump standing in an ornate gilded room (likely the White House) wearing a dark navy suit, light blue shirt, red tie and an American flag lapel pin, with a stern facial expression; the lighting is cinematic and shadowed. Centered in very large white serif all-caps is the slogan 'AMERICANS FIRST'. The lower half is an embedded line-chart card on a light background titled 'Total Native-Born Employment Level', with a legend distinguishing 'Biden Administration' (blue line) and 'Trump Administration (so far)' (red line). The x-axis runs Nov. 2023 through Nov. 2025, the y-axis 'Total employment (thousands)' from 129,000 to 134,000; a dashed vertical line labeled 'Inauguration' marks the transition, with the blue Biden line declining/flat and the red Trump line rising sharply afterward. The card is footed with 'THE WHITE HOUSE' and the seal appears at the very bottom. The messaging technique combines a strongman portrait, a patriotic slogan, and a partisan before/after chart to credit Trump with reversing native-born job losses; per the tweet it contrasts foreign-born job gains under Biden with native-born job gains under Trump. Target audience is the administration's base and general public, framing immigration restriction as protecting American-born workers.
notable_text: AMERICANS FIRST; Total Native-Born Employment Level; Biden Administration vs Trump Administration (so far); Inauguration; THE WHITE HOUSE
